title: Charset Registry
description: Connector resolves MySQL/MariaDB character set metadata for correct string decoding via a service-provider pattern

use_cases.name: Register Charset Service Provider
use_cases.description: Connector registers its specific CharsetRegistry ServiceProvider with the DefaultServiceRegistry at startup

use_cases.name: Initialize Charset Registry
use_cases.description: Registry lazily creates and initializes the charset service on first getService() call via multi-phase init (inject → configure → start)

use_cases.name: Load Charset Mappings from JSON
use_cases.description: MariaDbCharsetRegistry loads charset_mappings.json (Jackson) at construction, building three lookup maps: collationIndex→collationName, collationIndex→CharacterSetMapping, charsetName→CharacterSetMapping

use_cases.name: Resolve Collation Name for Collation Index
use_cases.description: Consumer retrieves the collation name string for a given numeric collation index

use_cases.name: Resolve Charset Name for Collation Index
use_cases.description: Consumer retrieves the database character set name for a given collation index

use_cases.name: Resolve Java Encoding for Charset Name
use_cases.description: Consumer retrieves the Java encoding string (e.g. UTF-8, Cp1252) for a named database charset, used to decode binary string data

use_cases.name: Get Charset Map Size
use_cases.description: Consumer queries the total number of entries in the charset map

key_classes.name: MySqlCharsetRegistry
key_classes.notes: Delegates to com.mysql.cj.CharsetMapping static methods; uses inner CharsetMappingMapper to expose protected getStaticJavaEncodingForMysqlCharset

key_classes.name: MariaDbCharsetRegistry
key_classes.notes: Self-contained; loads charset_mappings.json via Jackson into three maps at construction time; MAP_SIZE=1024

key_classes.name: DefaultServiceRegistry
key_classes.notes: Thread-safe (ConcurrentHashMap). Lazy init on getService(). Multi-phase init: injectDependencies → configureService → startService. close() stops services in reverse registration order.

use_case_relationships: MariaDBConnector -- UC3 (Load Charset Mappings from JSON) <<extend>>
BinlogFieldReader -- UC4 (Resolve Collation Name for Collation Index)
BinlogFieldReader -- UC5 (Resolve Charset Name for Collation Index)
BinlogFieldReader -- UC6 (Resolve Java Encoding for Charset Name)
important_notes: Two parallel implementations of the same BinlogCharsetRegistry interface: MySQL delegates to the MySQL JDBC driver's CharsetMapping, while MariaDB is fully self-contained using a bundled JSON mapping file (charset_mappings.json). Both are wired into the same ServiceRegistry pattern, so consumers (BinlogFieldReader) are decoupled from the connector-specific implementation.
